questions.q: When uploading a customer list to create a custom audience:
options: Meta receives the emails in plain text
The list is uploaded hashed and matching happens without exposing data in the clear
questions.explanation: Lists are hashed before being uploaded; Meta does matching with hashes, not with data in the clear.

options: The suitability level of the content the ad appears next to
questions.explanation: The inventory filter (expanded/moderate/limited) adjusts how strict the adjacent content is.

questions.q: Trade-off of stricter brand safety controls:
options: More brand safety but lower reach and possibly higher cost
questions.explanation: More safety reduces the available inventory: lower reach and sometimes higher cost.

questions.q: An ad says: 'We know you have back problems; this is for you'. It's rejected because:
options: It implies/asserts a personal attribute of the viewer
questions.explanation: The Advertising Standards prohibit implying a personal attribute of the user; the problem is the wording.

questions.q: What does the ad review process review?
options: Text, image, video, targeting and the destination landing page
questions.explanation: The ad review evaluates all the ad's components and its destination against the Advertising Standards.
